focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 17

The ready room was underground. The venue sat over Lena like a second pressure system, concrete and cooling ducts and forty thousand seats holding themselves above her head. In Kirchberg the pool had always sounded alive before she entered it: pumps, air handlers, the feed hiss in Margot's wrist unit, all the little system noises braided into one thing. Here the room was cleaner than that, almost ceremonial. Her own skull stayed cleaner still. There were no ambient calculations and no low hum of suggested timing, only silence where there had always been company.

Seven other swimmers waited with her. Lena knew most of them by shoulders, kick timing, and the habits that survived from meet to meet. Adelaïde Moreau stretched in the corner with the saint medallion she always tucked into her suit before the walk out. Kaede Watanabe had her eyes closed and her lips moving through some private rehearsal. Nina Okoro paced the far wall with three fingers taped bright green. The room held that smooth patched readiness Lena could still read before anyone touched the water, bodies already halfway into the race through systems she no longer trusted. Months ago she would have recognized it as ordinary. Tonight it looked slightly indecent, the body being helped in ways nobody in the seats would ever see.

Her towel lay across her knees. The terry cloth kept catching on the dry skin of her fingertips. She pulled the strap on one goggle, then the other, equal pressure, and made herself stop there. The old Lena would have been running invisible checklists without naming them, stroke count windows and turn timing and the fine adjustments a linked system offered so smoothly they had once felt like intuition. This version had to do the rude manual work. She took four controlle...
open_hooks_to_carry: Compressed time: most athletes signed Meridian's deal; Kai Lena and a few others run the partial patch or nothing
The meal: Yusuf's biryani, real conversation, laughter that is brief and genuine
Lena describes the silence in her head—learning to think without the system's ambient input
prev_tail_excerpt:
...ose chain on the side gate. She didn't flinch.

"Why not stop?" he said. "Not in general. Why not stop when it was Lena. When you knew what was happening to her."

Margot's jaw worked once. "Because by then I was already building my days around it. Because I could tell myself I had rules and that was bullshit. Because stopping meant being in my own body full time." She looked down at the pale strap mark without touching it. "My own body wasn't enough for me."

He let that sit between them. There wasn't a cleaner version under it. That was the worst part.

"Petra asked for a statement by five," Margot said after a while. "All the sessions where the chart and the telemetry don't match."

"Put Lena in it."

"I was going to."

"Put the missing laps in it. The days you wrote acceptable when they weren't. Every time you let the chart lie for you."

Her face tightened into a wince and flattened again. "Okay."

"And you stay away from her unless she asks for you."

"Okay."

The apology came after that, too late to redirect anything, too plain to be strategy.

"I'm sorry," Margot said. "About Lena. About Tomas. About all of it."

"Don't give that to me," Kai said. His voice had gone quiet enough that he barely recognized it as his. "That's not — I can't take that for them."

She nodded once. "All right."

He stood. The backs of his thighs were cold. The mountains stayed where they were, patient and indifferent.

He went back inside. Behind him, Margot stayed on the crate in the cold. He didn't look back to see whether she was still sitting there or whether Tomas's phantom had taken her leg again. The loading-dock door shut behind him. By five Petra would have it all in writing, every session where the chart and the body told different stories. Tomas's door would still be closed.
state_deltas_required: Time has passed and choices are made; what remains is the human baseline beneath the crisis
A moment of genuine human quiet before the public reckoning; the relationships are damaged but real
